preview_text:
The model favors Spain, assigning them a 48% win probability against France's 22%. This represents a substantial gap: Spain emerge as clear favorites on the forecast, while France are positioned as the underdog despite their historical standing in major tournaments.

The draw looms large in this matchup. At 30%, it ranks as the modal outcome across both model and market estimates, suggesting the teams are capable of canceling each other out. The model's spread—a 26-percentage-point gap between Spain and France—indicates Spain should control much of the match, yet the elevated draw probability implies competitive balance in execution rather than a runaway favorite scenario.

Model and market diverge markedly here. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign France roughly 41–42% win odds, nearly double the model's 22%, while crediting Spain only 29–30% compared to the model's 48%. This 19–26 percentage-point divergence across both outcomes is substantial. The dashboard makes no claim about which source is correct; it notes the disagreement for users to weigh independently.
